meta_title: Impact Doors in Hollywood, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Upgrade your Hollywood, FL home with HVHZ-rated impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. ESW & CWI brands, in-house install, 170mph wind protection. Free quote today!
h1: Hollywood's Most Trusted Impact Doors — Built for the Broadwalk's Toughest Storms
intro:
Living in Hollywood, Florida means waking up to ocean breezes, afternoon thunderstorms, and the ever-present reminder that South Florida's hurricane season is never truly off. From the waterfront homes along Hollywood Beach to the quiet tree-lined streets of Hollywood Lakes and the established neighborhoods of Emerald Hills, your home is a significant investment — one the average property in this city values at around $400,000. Protecting that investment starts at the most vulnerable points of any structure: its doors. Impact doors aren't just an upgrade here; in a city that sits just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coast inside Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, they are a necessity.

Hollywood homeowners face a unique combination of coastal exposure, aging housing stock, and some of the strictest building standards in the state. Nearly 55% of homes in Hollywood were built before 1994 — before the sweeping changes to the Florida Building Code triggered by Hurricane Andrew. That means a majority of entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors across neighborhoods like Hollywood Hills and Downtown Hollywood were never designed to withstand today's documented 170 mph wind speeds. Every hurricane season, these older doors become the weak link in an otherwise solid home. A single failed door can allow catastrophic pressure changes that lift roofs, flood interiors, and cause tens of thousands of dollars in structural damage — all in a matter of minutes.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — has spent years helping Hollywood families make the transition to true impact protection. Whether you're steps from the famous Hollywood Beach Broadwalk, a short drive from ArtsPark at Young Circle, or nestled in the quieter residential pockets near Dania Beach and Hallandale Beach, our team brings the same level of craftsmanship, compliance, and care to every installation. We carry ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact doors — two of the most respected brands in the South Florida market — and every job is handled entirely by our in-house team with 60+ years of combined experience. When storm season rolls around, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing homeowners don't board up. They lock up and sleep soundly.

section_why.h2: Why Hollywood Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of contractors willing to install impact doors in Hollywood, but there is a significant difference between a company that sells doors and a company that truly specializes in them. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the latter. With more than 500 homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our team has refined a process that prioritizes proper installation just as much as product quality. In a High Velocity Hurricane Zone like Hollywood, an impact door is only as strong as the frame it's mounted in and the methodology used to secure it — and that's where many contractors fall short.

One of our most important differentiators is that we are 100% in-house. We do not subcontract your installation to a third-party crew. The same licensed and insured professionals who walk through your home for the initial assessment are the ones who show up on installation day. This matters enormously in Hollywood's coastal environment, where saltwater exposure, humidity, and high wind pressure demand precision in every fastener, every sealant line, and every multi-point locking system alignment. Our technicians are intimately familiar with the City of Hollywood Building Division's permit requirements, ensuring your project is fully code-compliant from the first day to the final inspection.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau — a reflection of thousands of homeowner interactions done right, not just hundreds of completed projects. We understand that a Hollywood Lakes homeowner replacing their grand front entry door has different priorities than an Emerald Hills family upgrading a set of sliding glass doors overlooking their backyard pool. Our team approaches each project with that level of personalization, offering consultations that account for your home's architecture, your aesthetic preferences, and your protection goals.

Finally, we back our work with financing options that make sense for middle-income households across Hollywood. Starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, there's no reason to put off protection that could save you far more in storm damage, insurance premiums, and home value appreciation. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, service isn't a department — it's our identity.

section_history.h2: Hollywood's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Doors Every Hurricane Season
section_history.body:
Hollywood has been tested by nature more than most South Florida cities care to remember. Since 1990, the area has experienced at least 10 named storms or significant tropical events — but three stand out as defining moments for local homeowners. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 fundamentally changed how South Florida thinks about building codes, exposing catastrophic weaknesses in standard doors and windows across Broward and Miami-Dade counties. While Andrew's eye tracked south of Hollywood, its outer bands created devastating wind and surge conditions that left residents along Hollywood Beach and the surrounding coastal zone facing serious structural damage. Andrew's legacy is visible today in the city's building code standards — and in the 55% of Hollywood homes built before those standards were implemented.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a more direct blow. Wilma crossed South Florida as a Category 3 storm, hitting Broward County with sustained winds that shattered unprotected glass, tore off unsecured doors, and drove water into thousands of homes from Pembroke Pines to the Hollywood coastline. The storm was a stark reminder that no neighborhood — not the upscale homes near Hard Rock Hotel and Casino, not the mid-century bungalows of Downtown Hollywood — is immune to the destructive potential of a landfalling hurricane. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced that lesson again, bringing powerful wind gusts across the county even as its track shifted west.

The pattern is clear: Hollywood is not a city that gets near-misses indefinitely. Each season brings renewed risk, and each storm demonstrates that the difference between a home that weathers the storm and one that suffers significant damage often comes down to a single compromised entry point. Impact doors eliminate that vulnerability entirely — stopping wind, debris, and water intrusion before they can cascade into larger structural failures.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Hollywood
section_code.body:
Hollywood, Florida sits firmly within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone — a designation that applies to all of Broward County and carries the most demanding building standards in the state of Florida, and arguably in the entire United States. When it comes to impact doors, HVHZ compliance is not optional. The Florida Building Code requires that all exterior doors in the HVHZ meet Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) standards, which involve rigorous large-missile impact testing, cyclic wind pressure testing, and water infiltration resistance protocols. Products that pass these tests are certified to perform in wind conditions up to 170 mph — the documented design wind speed for Hollywood's wind zone.

For homeowners in Hollywood, this means that not every impact door on the market qualifies for legal installation. Only products carrying valid Miami-Dade NOA approvals or Florida Product Approvals that specify HVHZ use can be permitted and inspected through the City of Hollywood Building Division. Pulling the proper permit is a non-negotiable step, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on behalf of our clients. Our team prepares and submits all documentation, coordinates with the Building Division for inspections, and ensures your installation receives a final sign-off — giving you a clear paper trail for insurance purposes and any future home sale.

Beyond the doors themselves, HVHZ code also governs the installation methodology. Anchor spacing, fastener type and embedment depth, sill pan flashing, and threshold sealing all have specific requirements under the Florida Building Code that must be documented and inspected. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house installers are trained specifically on HVHZ standards and have completed projects across all Hollywood ZIP codes, from 33019 on the beach to 33024 further inland. When you work with us, compliance isn't a checkbox — it's built into every step of our process.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Hollywood's Coastal Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door brands available in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers engineer their products specifically for the demands of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, and both carry the Miami-Dade NOA certifications required for legal installation in Hollywood. Whether you're looking for a statement-making front entry door, a wide-span sliding glass door system for your pool deck, or elegant French doors that open to a courtyard, ESW and CWI offer configurations that meet Hollywood's code requirements without compromising on beauty or functionality.

ESW impact doors are particularly well-regarded for their structural integrity and thermal performance. Their multi-point locking systems engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, distributing force evenly during high-wind events and providing an exceptional level of security beyond storm protection. For Hollywood Beach homeowners dealing with the dual challenge of salt air corrosion and hurricane exposure, ESW's corrosion-resistant hardware and marine-grade finishing options are a significant advantage. CWI products are equally impressive, offering a broad selection of frame colors, glass options — including impact-laminated low-E glass that reduces heat gain — and door panel styles that complement both modern and traditional South Florida architecture.

For sliding glass door systems, both brands offer multi-panel configurations that can span large openings common in Hollywood's ranch-style and mid-century modern homes. These systems use interlocking panels with reinforced meeting stiles and heavy-duty rollers designed for smooth daily operation even after years of coastal exposure. Every product we install is rated to perform at Hollywood's 170 mph design wind speed, and every installation is backed by both the manufacturer's warranty and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's own workmanship guarantee. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying a door — you're investing in a complete protection system engineered for your specific environment.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost in Hollywood, FL — and How Quickly Do They Pay Off?
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in Hollywood varies depending on the number of openings, the door type, the size of the unit, and the complexity of the existing frame conditions. For a typical Hollywood home — say a three-bedroom, two-bath property in Emerald Hills or Hollywood Hills with a primary entry door and a set of sliding glass doors — homeowners can generally expect investment ranges that reflect both the premium quality of HVHZ-rated products and the precision labor required for compliant installation. Given that the average Hollywood home sits at approximately $400,000 in value, the return on a proper impact door installation is well-documented and often dramatic.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that investment accessible through flexible financing options, including plans starting at just $87 per month with $0 down required. For many Hollywood families on a middle-income budget, this approach turns what might feel like a large capital expenditure into a manageable monthly commitment — often one that is partially or fully offset by the insurance savings impact doors generate. When you factor in reduced storm damage risk, lower homeowner's insurance premiums, energy savings from thermally efficient glass, and the documented increase in resale value that impact-rated openings provide, the true cost of impact doors is substantially lower than the sticker price suggests.

It's also worth considering the cost of not upgrading. A single hurricane event that breaches an unprotected door can result in interior flooding, mold remediation, structural repairs, and contents losses that easily reach $20,000 to $50,000 or more — none of which is pleasant to navigate even with insurance coverage. Hollywood homeowners who have lived through Wilma or Irma understand this calculus intimately. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's financing ensures that protecting your home doesn't require waiting until the timing is perfect. We make it possible to act now, before the next storm has a name.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Hollywood Homeowner's Insurance Premiums with Impact Door Credits
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowner's insurance market is one of the most volatile in the nation, and Broward County — with its coastal exposure and HVHZ designation — carries some of the highest base premiums in the state. But Hollywood homeowners who install HVHZ-compliant impact doors have a meaningful tool for reducing those premiums: the wind mitigation credit. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounted premiums to policyholders who can demonstrate that their home has opening protections that meet Miami-Dade NOA standards. Impact doors, when properly documented, qualify for these credits — and the savings can be substantial.

After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and inspected by the City of Hollywood Building Division, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector. The resulting report documents your impact-rated openings in the standardized format that carriers like Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others use to calculate your premium credits. Depending on the percentage of openings protected and the rating level achieved, Hollywood homeowners commonly report annual savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars per year — savings that compound annually and meaningfully accelerate your return on investment.

Beyond the direct premium discount, impact doors also affect your hurricane deductible exposure. Many policies in Broward County carry hurricane deductibles expressed as a percentage of insured value — often 2% to 5%. On a $400,000 home, that's an $8,000 to $20,000 out-of-pocket threshold before coverage kicks in following a named storm. By reducing the likelihood that your home sustains interior damage in the first place, impact doors effectively reduce the probability that you'll ever have to meet that deductible. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team is happy to walk you through the documentation process and help you get the most out of your wind mitigation report.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors if I'm not right on the Hollywood Beach waterfront?
faqs.a: Yes. The High Velocity Hurricane Zone designation applies to all of Hollywood and all of Broward County, regardless of how close you are to the water. Whether you live in Hollywood Hills, Emerald Hills, or near the Anne Kolb Nature Center, your home is subject to the same 170 mph design wind speed requirements. HVHZ-rated doors with Miami-Dade NOA approval are required for legal permit approval anywhere in the city, and they provide critical protection against both wind pressure and windborne debris for every neighborhood in Hollywood.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Hollywood, and what financing does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offer?
faqs.a: The total cost depends on the number of openings, door types, sizes, and installation complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available, making impact doors accessible for most Hollywood homeowners without requiring a large upfront payment. When you factor in insurance premium savings, energy efficiency improvements, and the potential to avoid costly storm damage, most homeowners find the net investment is far lower than the initial price suggests. We provide detailed, transparent quotes during your free consultation.

faqs.q: Does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle the permit process with the City of Hollywood Building Division?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf, from document preparation and submission to coordinating final inspections with the City of Hollywood Building Division. We ensure all products installed carry valid Miami-Dade NOA approvals appropriate for HVHZ use, and that all installation methodology complies with current Florida Building Code standards. You receive a fully permitted, inspected installation — which is essential for insurance documentation, code compliance, and protecting your home's resale value.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Hollywood home?
faqs.a: For most Hollywood homes, a standard impact door installation — covering a front entry door and one or two additional openings — can typically be completed in one to two days of on-site work. Larger projects involving multiple sliding glass doors, French doors, and entry systems may require additional time. The permitting process through the City of Hollywood Building Division adds some lead time before the physical installation begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will give you a clear project timeline during your consultation so you know exactly what to expect from start to final inspection.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Hollywood, and by how much?
faqs.a: Yes — Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation credits for homes with HVHZ-compliant impact openings. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is inspected and approved, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your impact doors in the format carriers require. Hollywood homeowners in Broward County commonly see annual premium reductions ranging from a few hundred dollars to over $1,000 depending on the percentage of openings protected and your current carrier. These savings accumulate every year, significantly improving your long-term return on investment.
